title: Gynopogon ruscifolius
intro: Gynopogon ruscifolius, also known as the "Viper's Grass" or "Ruscus," is a species of flowering plant in the Asparagaceae family, native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Gynopogon ruscifolius include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions and support overall well-being.
taxonomy: Gynopogon ruscifolius is classified in the family Costaceae. It belongs to the order Zingiberales, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the gingers. The family Costaceae is a small family of plants that are native to tropical regions. Gynopogon ruscifolius is one of the approximately 10 species within the genus Gynopogon. This classification is based on the plant's physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
